comment_text.sn6.13:1.1: The Buddha taught Ānanda there (AN 5.114). It lay to the west of Rājagaha on the road from Varanasi. Once, arriving there from Varanasi, the Buddha allowed porridge (Kd 6:24.1.1, cf. AN 5.207). At Kd 2:12.1.1, Mahākassapa was swept away in a river while returning from Andhakavinda. That river is identified by the commentary as the Serpentine, but that is usually said to be the Panchane, which lies to the east of Rājagaha. Perhaps the river near Andhakavinda was, rather, the Paimar, which lies about three hours walk to the west of Rājagaha.
comment_text.sn6.13:2.1: This verse was spoken by Mahācunda at Thag 2.11:2.1.
comment_text.sn6.13:5.4: Maccuhāyi is glossed by the commentaries as either “abandoner” (maccuhāyinanti maraṇapariccāginaṁ) or “conqueror” (maraṇābhibhū maccuhāyī). The phrase seems to be used in martial contexts where it carries a similar force to jetvā māraṁ just below.
comment_text.sn6.13:6.1: “More than five hundred” is the count of stream-enterers also at SN 55.10:3.3 = DN 18:1.7 = DN 16:2.7.15; see too MN 73:7.3 ff. and MN 76:53.2.
comment_text.sn6.13:6.2: The ca here indicates the numbers should be added, totaling 1,500.

root_text.sn6.13:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā māgadhesu viharati andhakavinde.
root_text.sn6.13:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā rattandhakāratimisāyaṁ abbhokāse nisinno hoti, devo ca ekamekaṁ phusāyati.
root_text.sn6.13:1.3: Atha kho brahmā sahampati abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇo kevalakappaṁ andhakavindaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn6.13:1.4: Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho brahmā sahampati bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn6.13:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Magadhans at Andhakavinda.
translation_text.sn6.13:1.2: Now at that time the Buddha was meditating in the open during the dark of night, while a gentle rain drizzled down.
translation_text.sn6.13:1.3: Then, late at night, the glorious divinity Sahampati, lighting up the entirety of Andhakavinda, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side,
translation_text.sn6.13:1.4: and recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn6.13:2.2: and practice to be released from fetters.
translation_text.sn6.13:2.3: If you don’t find enjoyment there,
translation_text.sn6.13:2.4: live in the Saṅgha, self-protected and mindful.
translation_text.sn6.13:3.1: Walking for alms from family to family,
translation_text.sn6.13:3.2: with senses guarded, alert and mindful.
translation_text.sn6.13:3.4: free of fear, freed in the fearless.
translation_text.sn6.13:4.2: where the lightning flashes and the heavens thunder
translation_text.sn6.13:4.3: in the dark of the night;
translation_text.sn6.13:4.4: there meditates a mendicant, free of goosebumps.
translation_text.sn6.13:5.1: For this is ever seen by me,
translation_text.sn6.13:5.2: it isn’t just the testament of hearsay.
translation_text.sn6.13:5.4: there are a thousand conquerors of Death.
translation_text.sn6.13:6.1: And of trainees, more than five hundred,
translation_text.sn6.13:6.4: freed from rebirth in the animal realm.
translation_text.sn6.13:7.1: And as for the rest of folk
translation_text.sn6.13:7.2: who I think have shared in merit—

variant_text.sn6.13:4.1: sarīsapā → siriṁsapā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn6.13:6.1: Bhiyyo → bhīyo (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
